metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete grep-based proving command to AC4 that confirms each enumerated Tier-2 symbol is absent and each retained symbol is present, e.g. `grep -rn '_RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK' src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_graph.py | grep -c '' | grep -q '^0$'` (or a script that runs all such checks and exits non-zero on any failure).
evidence: AC4: 'Exactly the enumerated Tier-2 symbols removed; each verified unreferenced; retained symbols untouched.'
AC6: 'make lint + mypy clean; the touched test modules pass in-session.'
ABSENCE: no grep or AST-search command is given to confirm each symbol is absent post-change.
findings.finding: AC4 ('Exactly the enumerated Tier-2 symbols removed; each verified unreferenced; retained symbols untouched') has no stated proving command. The umbrella AC6 ('make lint + mypy clean; the touched test modules pass in-session') does NOT prove symbol removal: mypy and lint will not report an error for a symbol that was already dead and is now simply absent — they pass whether or not the removals happened.
scenarios: An agent removes only some symbols, AC6 still passes (lint/mypy are green), and AC4 is incorrectly declared done.
A retained symbol is accidentally removed; mypy/lint do not catch the omission if the symbol was already locally unused.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'Verified by: `python scripts/verify_symbol_removals.py` (or equivalent per-file grep assertions) exits 0 — each dead symbol returns 0 hits; each retained symbol returns ≥1 hit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the AI-3b scope: the exact validator/function under test, the `category: transform` input fixture shape, and the assertion that proves category-agnosticism (e.g. 'call `validate_prompt_write(…, category="transform")` and assert it does not raise the same error as `category: review` would have').
evidence: Plan text: 'test_validation_depth.py (AI-3b): add a non-review (category: transform) test proving input-contract validation is category-agnostic.'
File test_validation_depth.py does not exist anywhere under tests/ — confirmed by directory listing and search.
No validator function, input fixture, or assertion is named; the decision rule for what constitutes 'category-agnostic input-contract validation' is entirely unspecified.

Cached plan-review finding corroborates: '.rebar/cache/plan-review/62a3-a49d-04a0-4f18/66e16a547a2900ac4d7f.json' notes 'AI-3b scope does not specify which validator function to call, what inputs to pass, or what assertion to make in the new category: transform test, requiring the executor to infer the full test.'
findings.finding: The AI-3b scope specifies adding a test to 'test_validation_depth.py' (a file that does not exist in the repo) proving 'input-contract validation is category-agnostic', but provides no processing protocol: it names neither the validator function under test, the inputs to supply, nor the assertion to make. An executor cannot implement this test without inferring the entire test body from a single-sentence description.
findings.location: Scope section: 'test_validation_depth.py (AI-3b)' and AC line '[ ] AI-3b non-review category test added and passing.'
scenarios: An implementor creates test_validation_depth.py but tests a different validator or uses a different assertion than was intended, silently satisfying the AC while missing the real regression coverage.
Two implementors produce incompatible tests for the same AC line with no specification to arbitrate between them.

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AI-1 fix to `run_differs._read_local_ticket_full` (line 159): apply the same explicit env construction (strip REBAR_SYNC_PULL on the live path; set it off on the no_sync path) and add a corresponding regression test.
evidence: Plan scope: 'reconcile_helpers.py (AI-1): when no_sync=False, _env=None inherits the parent env' — only reconcile_helpers.py is named
run_differs.py line 159: `_env = dict(_os.environ, REBAR_SYNC_PULL='off') if no_sync else None` — identical pattern
run_differs.py line 679: `local_reader=lambda lid: _read_local_ticket_full(repo_root, lid, no_sync=not persist)` — called on the live production path
findings.finding: run_differs._read_local_ticket_full has the identical AI-1 env-inheritance bug (line 159: `_env = dict(_os.environ, REBAR_SYNC_PULL='off') if no_sync else None`) as reconcile_helpers._read_local_tickets, but is NOT named as a co-target of the fix. An ambient REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off in the caller's environment would still suppress the live sync pull via run_differs, leaving the defect present on one of the two production call-sites.
scenarios: A caller sets REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off in their environment and calls the reconciler. reconcile_helpers is fixed to strip it, but run_differs._read_local_ticket_full still inherits it and suppresses the live sync pull — the bug is only half-fixed.
findings.suggested_fix: Apply the same explicit-env pattern to run_differs.py line 159: `_env = {k: v for k, v in _os.environ.items() if k != 'REBAR_SYNC_PULL'}` on the live path, and `dict(_os.environ, REBAR_SYNC_PULL='off')` on the no_sync path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AI-1 fix and its regression test to also strip REBAR_NO_SYNC (the permanent alias) from the constructed env on the live path, or explicitly assert that REBAR_NO_SYNC is also neutralized.
evidence: AC: 'regression test asserts the live path strips an ambient REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off' — only REBAR_SYNC_PULL is mentioned, REBAR_NO_SYNC is absent
src/rebar/_deprecations.py:80: `_permanent('env', 'REBAR_NO_SYNC', 'REBAR_SYNC_PULL')` — permanent alias
src/rebar/_config_sources.py:233: `'REBAR_NO_SYNC': ('sync', 'pull', 'REBAR_SYNC_PULL')` — resolved through the config layer
findings.finding: The AI-1 fix constructs an explicit env for the subprocess but does not strip REBAR_NO_SYNC (the permanent alias for REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off, as documented in src/rebar/_deprecations.py:80 and _config_sources.py:233). On the live path, an ambient REBAR_NO_SYNC=1 in the caller's environment is inherited by the subprocess and resolves through the rebar config layer to sync.pull='off', silently suppressing the live sync pull even after the fix.
findings.location: Tier 1 / AI-1 — src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile_helpers.py:387 (and the AC: 'live path strips an ambient REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off')
scenarios: A CI runner sets REBAR_NO_SYNC=1 in the environment. The fix strips REBAR_SYNC_PULL but leaves REBAR_NO_SYNC. The subprocess's rebar config layer resolves REBAR_NO_SYNC to sync.pull=off, and the live sync pull is still suppressed.
findings.suggested_fix: In the env construction, strip both keys: `_env = {k: v for k, v in _os.environ.items() if k not in ('REBAR_SYNC_PULL', 'REBAR_NO_SYNC')}` on the live path. Update the AC to cover both.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AI-1 live-path env construction to also delete `REBAR_NO_SYNC` from the explicit env dict (mirroring the REBAR_SYNC_PULL strip), and add a regression assertion that an ambient `REBAR_NO_SYNC=1` is also stripped on the live path.
evidence: src/rebar/_config_sources.py:276-279 — env_overrides() checks REBAR_NO_SYNC in os.environ independently of REBAR_SYNC_PULL
src/rebar/_deprecations.py:80 — _permanent('env', 'REBAR_NO_SYNC', 'REBAR_SYNC_PULL') — permanent alias
Plan scope: 'strip the key on the live path' — singular; only REBAR_SYNC_PULL is named
AC: 'live path strips an ambient REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off' — REBAR_NO_SYNC not mentioned
findings.finding: The AI-1 live-path fix strips REBAR_SYNC_PULL from the subprocess env but does not also strip REBAR_NO_SYNC (the permanent alias). The subprocess's _config_sources.env_overrides() reads REBAR_NO_SYNC independently from os.environ; an ambient REBAR_NO_SYNC=1 in the caller's environment therefore still suppresses the live pull even after the fix. The AC regression test spec only mentions 'REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off', so this residual bypass path goes untested.
findings.location: Scope / AI-1 (reconcile_helpers.py); AC line 'the live path strips an ambient REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off'
scenarios: A CI job sets REBAR_NO_SYNC=1 (the old env name) to speed up local runs; the reconciler's live subprocess inherits it and silently skips the sync pull even after the AI-1 fix is applied.
findings.suggested_fix: On the live path, build: `_env = {k: v for k, v in _os.environ.items() if k not in ('REBAR_SYNC_PULL', 'REBAR_NO_SYNC')}`. Add a parallel AC assertion: `monkeypatch.setenv('REBAR_NO_SYNC', '1'); assert env['REBAR_NO_SYNC'] not in subprocess_env`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `run_differs._read_local_ticket_full` (run_differs.py:159) as a co-target of the AI-1 fix; apply the same explicit-env construction and strip logic, and cover it in the regression test.
evidence: run_differs.py:159 — `_env = dict(_os.environ, REBAR_SYNC_PULL='off') if no_sync else None`
run_differs.py:679 — `local_reader=lambda lid: _read_local_ticket_full(repo_root, lid, no_sync=not persist)`
Plan scope text: 'reconcile_helpers.py (AI-1)' — run_differs.py not named
ABSENCE: run_differs.py does not appear in scope, Tier-2 list, or Retained list
findings.finding: run_differs._read_local_ticket_full (run_differs.py:159) contains the identical `_env = dict(_os.environ, REBAR_SYNC_PULL='off') if no_sync else None` pattern as reconcile_helpers.py:387 and is called with `no_sync=not persist` (line 679), so on the live persist=True path it also inherits the full parent env. The plan's AI-1 scope names only reconcile_helpers.py, leaving this parallel site with the same defect.
scenarios: A production reconcile pass with persist=True and REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off in the parent environment: reconcile_helpers.py is fixed but run_differs._read_local_ticket_full still inherits the suppression, silently skipping the tickets-branch fetch during the binding-walk phase.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out statement for the AI-1 behavioral change (e.g., 'back out by reverting the Gerrit CL; no data migration required') to satisfy T4's reversibility requirement.
evidence: 'when no_sync=False, _env=None inherits the parent env, so a caller's REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off silently suppresses the live sync pull'
'Build an explicit env; set REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off only when no_sync=True; strip the key on the live path.'
AC1: 'regression test asserts the live path strips an ambient REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off' — no back-out plan stated
ABSENCE: no 'to roll back, revert ...' or expand-contract sequencing mentioned anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: The AI-1 behavioral change (explicitly stripping REBAR_SYNC_PULL from the live-path env) alters observable runtime behavior of reconcile_helpers.py. The plan justifies the fix and adds a regression test, but provides no explicit rollback/back-out statement (e.g., 'revert the Gerrit change' or an expand-contract step). T4 requires more than acknowledgement — it requires the reversibility mechanism to be stated.
findings.location: Scope / AI-1 bullet; Acceptance Criteria AC1
scenarios: The env-stripping logic interacts unexpectedly with a downstream consumer that deliberately sets REBAR_SYNC_PULL in the parent env for a reason not captured by the grep survey; without a stated rollback path the operator has no documented recovery procedure.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection under AI-1: 'Back-out: revert this Gerrit change; the env-stripping logic is isolated to reconcile_helpers._build_env; no schema or data migration is involved.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one test each for DefaultBatchRunner.run() and CodeReviewBatchRunner.run() called without agent_runner (i.e., using the new None default) to verify they either handle None gracefully or raise a clear, expected error — ensuring the newly-defaulted signature is exercised at runtime, not just type-checked.
evidence: 'make agent_runner optional in the base BatchRunner, DefaultBatchRunner, and CodeReviewBatchRunner too'
'ProductionBatchRunner already takes agent_runner optional (agent_runner=None, tested via a 1-arg call)' — only ProductionBatchRunner's path is evidenced as tested
AC for this item is purely 'mypy clean', satisfiable without exercising DefaultBatchRunner(no agent_runner) or CodeReviewBatchRunner(no agent_runner) at runtime
findings.finding: DefaultBatchRunner.run and CodeReviewBatchRunner.run gain a new optional default (agent_runner=None) but the plan cites no test verifying these classes handle agent_runner=None without crashing; the sole cited test is a pre-existing ProductionBatchRunner 1-arg call, which doesn't cover the newly-defaulted path in the other two classes.
findings.location: Tier 1 — runners.py + code_review/batch_runner.py (#450); AC: 'BatchRunner.run signatures aligned; mypy clean'
scenarios: DefaultBatchRunner.run(runner) with no agent_runner calls internal code that dereferences agent_runner without a None guard, silently crashing at runtime while the test suite stays green
CodeReviewBatchRunner receives agent_runner=None and passes it to an API that expects a live agent, producing a confusing AttributeError instead of a clear contract violation
findings.suggested_fix: Add two focused unit tests (one per class) that call the method with agent_runner omitted and assert the expected behavior (e.g., raises TypeError with a descriptive message if None is not operationally valid, or succeeds if None is handled). Reference these tests in the AC alongside the mypy requirement.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit `if agent_runner is None: raise TypeError("DefaultBatchRunner requires a non-None agent_runner")` guard (and equivalently in CodeReviewBatchRunner) so the None case fails fast with a clear message, rather than crashing mid-batch with AttributeError.
evidence: runners.py:153 — `out = agent_runner.run(ctx).outputs or {}` — unconditional call in DefaultBatchRunner
batch_runner.py:73 — `out = agent_runner.run(ctx).outputs or {}` — unconditional call in CodeReviewBatchRunner
production_batch_runner.py:69-73 — `agent_runner: AgentStepRunner | None = None` then `del agent_runner` — safe because unused
Plan: 'make agent_runner optional in the base BatchRunner, DefaultBatchRunner, and CodeReviewBatchRunner' — no guard specified
findings.finding: Making `agent_runner` optional (`agent_runner=None`) in `DefaultBatchRunner.run` and `CodeReviewBatchRunner.run` for mypy override consistency introduces a latent AttributeError: both implementations unconditionally call `agent_runner.run(ctx)` (runners.py:153, batch_runner.py:73), so a caller that relies on the new default and omits agent_runner gets a silent `NoneType has no attribute 'run'` crash at runtime. Unlike `ProductionBatchRunner` (which does `del agent_runner` immediately), these two runners genuinely require a non-None agent_runner. The plan specifies no guard or early assertion.
findings.location: Scope / runners.py + code_review/batch_runner.py (#450); AC line 'BatchRunner.run signatures aligned; mypy clean'
scenarios: A new workflow author sees the base class signature `run(req, agent_runner=None)` and constructs `DefaultBatchRunner().run(req)` (omitting agent_runner) — crashes at runtime on the first batch with AttributeError rather than a clear TypeError.
findings.suggested_fix: At the top of DefaultBatchRunner.run and CodeReviewBatchRunner.run, add: `if agent_runner is None: raise TypeError(f"{type(self).__name__}.run requires a non-None agent_runner")`.

findings.finding: The final AC bundles 'LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1' as a completion criterion without the [operator-attested] tag, making it appear as an in-session completable check when it actually requires external human reviewer action that an executing agent cannot perform or verify in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria / final bullet: 'make lint + mypy clean; touched test modules pass; single Gerrit change lands only after LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the last AC bullet to: `- [operator-attested] Single Gerrit change lands only after LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1 (record change-id and vote timestamp here).`

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
